name: La Muela de Cortes de Pallás — Circular from Jalance
name_original: La Muela de Cortes de Pallás — Circular desde Jalance
region_intro: Road cycling routes around Jalance traverse a diverse landscape characterized by the Júcar River Gorge and the rugged Caroig Massif. The region features varied terrains, including both paved roads and unpaved tracks, with significant elevation changes. Cyclists can expect a hilly topography with long climbs and descents, offering a challenging experience. This area provides a dynamic environment for road cycling, blending natural features with historical sites. Best road cycling routes around Jalance The most popular road cycling route is La Muela de Cortes de Pallás — Circular from Jalance, a 49.9 miles (80.2 km) trail that takes 5 hours 18 minutes to complete. This difficult route features substantial elevation gain, making it suitable for experienced cyclists. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Fuente del Jarral – Cofrentes Castle loop from Jalance, a difficult 75.9 miles (122.2 km) path. This extensive route offers a demanding ride through the region's varied terrain, including significant ascents. Local road cyclists also love the Fuente del Jarral – Zarra loop from Jalance, a 36.1 miles (58.1 km) trail leading through the local countryside, often completed in about 2 hours 41 minutes. Road cycling around Jalance is defined by river valleys, mountainous areas, and varied terrains within the Caroig Massif. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate to difficult. The routes in Jalance are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.3 stars from over 4 reviews. More than 400 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Jalance's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many road cycling routes are available around Jalance?
A: There are over 25 road cycling routes around Jalance, offering a diverse range of experiences. The region is known for its varied terrain, including both paved roads and unpaved tracks, with significant elevation changes.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on road cycling routes in Jalance?
A: Road cycling in Jalance features a dynamic environment with river valleys, mountainous areas, and varied terrains within the Caroig Massif. You can expect a hilly topography with long climbs and descents, providing a challenging experience for cyclists.

Q: Are there routes suitable for advanced road cyclists in Jalance?
A: Yes, Jalance is particularly well-suited for advanced road cyclists. Out of the 28 available routes, 20 are classified as difficult, featuring substantial elevation gains and demanding distances. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20132414" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Fuente del Jarral – Cofrentes Castle loop from Jalance&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 122.2 km path with over 2,400 meters of ascent.

Q: Are there any circular road cycling routes in Jalance?
A: Many of the road cycling routes in Jalance are circular, allowing you to start and end in the same location. An example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e840081796/la-muela-de-cortes-de-pallas-circular-from-jalance" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Muela de Cortes de Pallás — Circular from Jalance&lt;/a&gt;, which is an 80.2 km loop with significant elevation gain.

Q: What natural features can I see along road cycling routes in Jalance?
A: The routes often traverse the dramatic Júcar River Gorge and the rugged Caroig Massif. You might encounter natural monuments such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3178996" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Corbinet Waterfall&lt;/a&gt;, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3216823" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Arroyo de Cortes Waterfall&lt;/a&gt;, or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4963542" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Confluence of the Cabriel and Júcar rivers&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any historical landmarks or castles to explore near the cycling routes?
A: Yes, the region blends natural beauty with historical sites. Cyclists can pass by or visit landmarks such as &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2751423" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Chirel Castle&lt;/a&gt;, which offers incredible views of the Muela de Cortes and the Júcar river, or &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4578187" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cofrentes Castle&lt;/a&gt;. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5570151" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Jalance Castle&lt;/a&gt; is also a notable historical point.

Q: Are there any wild swimming spots near the road cycling routes in Jalance?
A: The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3178996" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Corbinet Waterfall&lt;/a&gt; is a notable recreational area near some routes where visitors can swim in crystal-clear waters, especially on warm days. The Júcar River itself also offers opportunities for refreshing stops.

Q: What do other road cyclists say about the routes in Jalance?
A: The road cycling routes in Jalance are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.3 stars from over 4 reviews. More than 400 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Jalance's varied terrain, often praising the challenging climbs and scenic landscapes.

Q: What is the best time of year for road cycling in Jalance?
A: Jalance, situated in the province of Valencia, Spain, generally offers favorable conditions for road cycling for much of the year. Spring and autumn typically provide pleasant temperatures, while summer can be warm, making early morning or late afternoon rides ideal. Winters are usually mild.

Q: Are there any family-friendly road cycling routes in Jalance?
A: While many routes in Jalance feature significant elevation changes and are rated moderate to difficult, there are some options that might be more suitable for families or those seeking less strenuous rides. It's best to check individual route details for specific difficulty levels and terrain to find the most appropriate option.

Q: Is parking available for cyclists in Jalance?
A: Jalance is a small town, and parking is generally available within the town center or near common starting points for routes. Specific parking information for individual routes can often be found in the route descriptions on komoot.

Q: Can I reach Jalance by public transport with my bike?
A: While public transport options exist to reach Jalance, bringing a bike can sometimes be challenging depending on the service. It's advisable to check with local bus or train operators regarding their policies for transporting bicycles before planning your trip.

Q: Are road cycling permits required in Jalance?
A: Generally, no specific permits are required for road cycling on public roads and established routes around Jalance. However, if you plan to venture into protected natural areas or private land, it's always wise to check for any specific regulations or access restrictions.

Q: Are there cafes or accommodation options for cyclists in Jalance?
A: Jalance offers local amenities including cafes where you can refuel, and various accommodation options such as guesthouses or rural lodgings suitable for cyclists. These can provide a comfortable base for exploring the region's routes.

Q: Which route offers a moderate challenge for road cyclists?
A: For a moderate challenge, consider the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31711422" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Fuente del Jarral – Zarra loop from Jalance&lt;/a&gt;. This 58.1 km trail leads through the local countryside and can typically be completed in about 2 hours 41 minutes, offering a good balance of distance and elevation.
tips: Asphalted road in very good condition with beautiful views on each curve towards the Cofrentes-Ayora Valley. It allows you to get from La Cañada de Abajo to the Alto del Cinto de la Cabra, overcoming a difference in level of 500m.
A panoramic point with beautiful views of the reservoir and the Cortes mill and in the distance you can also see the town. It is located next to a hut that serves as a shelter, viewpoint and to rest.

It is located next to the road and has a drinking water fountain, grills and picnic tables. To all this add the amazing views of the Cortes reservoir with its characteristic turquoise color of its waters. A good place to rest or recharge with water.
